findings.evidence: `rg -n "normalizerQuotientConjEquiv_trans|subgroup_map_conj_trans|conjMulEquivTrans"` shows only the iterated-target lemma at this file line 82, while `Deck/Conjugation.lean:165` and `:181` already provide the composite conjugation and subgroup-map equalities this API should use.
findings.fix: Add a deck-level congruence lemma, e.g. `normalizerQuotientConjEquiv_trans_mk_congr`, using `Subgroup.normalizerQuotientEquivMap_trans_mk_congr`, `Deck.subgroup_map_conj_trans`, and `Deck.conjMulEquivTrans` to state the representative formula against the composite over-base homeomorphism.
findings.issue: `normalizerQuotientConjEquiv_trans_mk` only rewrites to `normalizerQuotient ((H.map conj h).map conj k)` and does not expose the characteristic comparison with `normalizerQuotientConjEquiv (h.trans k)`, so deck consumers must manually combine subgroup casts and conjugation composition.

summary: The algebraic transport API is mostly characterized, but the deck-facing public API still leaves composition at the iterated mapped subgroup instead of the composite cover isomorphism needed by the stated downstream target.
